Villain (Flash in BB) has a history of calling frequently and passively postflop. With pot of 600 and deep stacks near tournament end, bet half pot (300) to get called by worse hands like weaker jack, tens, sevens, or draws.

I have middle pair (sevens) with a King kicker on a coordinated board of Ts-7h-Js. Elon's continuation bet of 300 into a 600 pot is a standard sizing. While the board is heavy with draws and higher cards, middle pair is too strong to fold to a single bet in a heads-up match. Raising would likely only get called by hands that beat me, so calling is the best way to keep the pot controlled, see a turn card, and allow Elon to continue with bluffs.

The turn brings a second Jack (Jd), pairing the board. My hand (Kc 7d) has now become two pair, Jacks and Sevens, with a King kicker. However, this is still a marginal hand in this context. Any hand with a Jack now has trips, and any hand with a Ten (which was already beating me) still beats me. The board is also very coordinated with several straight and flush draws. Checking is the most prudent action here to control the size of the pot and see how Elon reacts. If he checks behind, it's likely I have the best hand or he is also wary of the paired board. If he bets, I will have to evaluate the sizing and the likelihood of him having improved or continuing a bluff.

Turn board pairs the jacks, giving me trips with Qh kicker (Qc would block but irrelevant). This is an extremely strong hand, near the nuts, crushing villain's likely range of top pair (T or worse Jx), two pair (TJ, J7), sets (77, TT unlikely given preflop action), or draws (spade flush, 89 str). Villain checked again as BB OOP, consistent with passive history of calling flops/turns frequently but folding to heavy aggression sometimes late. Pot 1200, deep stacks (15k effective), 15 hands left: value bet ~3/4 pot (900) to get called by all worse made hands and draws while building pot for river shove if called. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or letting villain realize equity. Aggression maximizes EV against calling station.

I am in the Big Blind with Kc 7d. The board is Ts 7h Js Jd. I have two pair (Jacks and Sevens) with a King kicker. Pre-flop, I called a standard button raise. On the flop, I check-called a 50% pot bet with middle pair. The turn Jd is an interesting card; it pairs the board, which theoretically makes it less likely Elon holds a Jack, though any Jack now has trips. Elon's bet of 900 into 1200 (75% pot) is a strong sizing, suggesting either a very strong hand (like a Jack, a Ten, or a slow-played overpair) or a polarized bluff/semi-bluff (like a spade flush draw or a straight draw). In heads-up play, folding a pair of sevens here is likely too exploitable, especially since the pairing card (the Jack) is a card I'm already losing to if he has it. I will call to see a river and evaluate based on the final card and his action.

On the river, the 8s is a very dangerous card as it completes both the spade flush and several straight possibilities (any hand with a 9). My hand, Jacks and Sevens, is now quite weak as any player holding a 10 or an 8 now has a better two-pair, and any Jack has trips. Given Elon's strong betting on the turn (75% of the pot), it's highly likely he has a hand that beats me or was semi-bluffing a draw that has now potentially arrived. Betting myself would not be for value, so checking is the only logical move. I will check and then evaluate his action, though a large bet from him on this specific runout would likely force a fold.

River pairs no board cards but completes possible spade flushes (Ts Js 8s) and straights like 98 (789T J). My Jc gives trips (JJJ) with Q kicker, extremely strong, ahead of villain's likely range of weaker pairs (T, Jx worse kicker), two pairs (T8? J7/T7 unlikely), missed draws. Villain (Flash) checked three streets postflop, consistent with passive calling station tendencies from history (calls flops/turns/rivers frequently with medium strength). Preflop OOP call, flop call into c-bet, turn call 900 OOP into ~1800 pot shows capped range without nuts. Pot 3000, my stack 14150 effective, 14 hands left: shoving maximizes value as villain likely calls with worse (e.g., T, weak J, flush draws that hit? unlikely check, but stations call down). Checking gives up value; smaller bet risks check-behind or fold. Per turn plan: build for river shove. High EV shove against calling range.

On the river, the 8s is a catastrophic card for my specific hand. It completes the spade flush and multiple straights (any 9x hand). Furthermore, any hand containing a Ten or an Eight now provides Elon with a superior two-pair to my Jacks and Sevens. Elon's bet of 14,150 into a roughly 3,000 pot is a massive overbet shove (nearly 5x the pot). In this tournament context, such a large bet on a highly coordinated board is almost always polarzied towards extreme value (trips, flushes, or straights) rather than a bluff. My hand is now at the very bottom of my calling range and cannot reasonably expect to be good against this sizing. With the tournament nearing its end (Hand 136 of 150) and a healthy stack, I will fold and wait for a better spot to preserve my lead.
I have Ace-Queen Offsuit on the button, which is a very strong hand in heads-up play. I will raise to 300 to build the pot and exert pressure, as is my standard opening size in this tournament.

Heads-up in late stages (hand 137/150), deep stacks (17k effective). Button (Flash) raises to 300, standard wide range ~40-50% from SB/Button. K3o has King blocker reducing villain's Kx value hands, decent high card equity (~35-40% vs wide range), excellent pot odds (200 to call into 400, 3:1, need ~25%). Calling realizes equity postflop with position playability; 3-bet bluff not ideal with offsuit non-connector; folding too tight in BB defense spot. Plan to c/r or check-fold weak flops.

Heads-up on flop, OOP in BB with K<spades>3<clubs>, completely missing the 7<spades>7<hearts>6<hearts> paired board with heart draw. No pair, no straight draw (no gutshot even), no flush draw. Pure air with king-high showdown value at best, but villain's button opening range (~40-50%: Ax, Kx, QJ+, suited connectors, pairs) hits this board decently (sets like 77/76/67 unlikely but possible, two-pair 76/67, flush draws, overpairs). Preflop call was fine for pot odds and implied odds/position postflop, but this is a clear check-fold spot. Donk betting OOP is -EV with no equity; check-raise bluff blocked poorly (king blocker ok for AK/KQ but offsuit 3 hurts straight blockers). Checking preserves stack late in tournament (137/150), induces bluffs or check-backs from missed draws/overcards; fold to any reasonable c-bet (~300-600). Maximizes EV by avoiding spew, waits for better spots with 14 hands left and deep stacks.

On a paired board of 7s-7h-6h, I have Ace-high with the Ace of clubs. As the pre-flop aggressor, a continuation bet is standard here to protect my equity against overcards and draws (like straight draws or flush draws). Given the board texture, a half-pot bet is sufficient to find out where I stand or take the pot down immediately. If Elon has a 7, he will likely call or check-raise, but I cannot give a free card to potential draws.

Heads-up on a paired 7s7h6h flop, checked to villain's button who bets 1/3 pot (300 into 900). Holding Ks3c: pure king-high air with zero draws, no pair, gutshot, or flush potential. Preflop defend was fine with position postflop and pot odds, but this flop heavily favors villain's wide button range (sets 77/76/67 rare but crushing, overpairs QQ+, TpTK like A7/K7/Q7, flush draws Khxh/8h5h/etc., str8 draws 89/58/45 suited). Ks blocks some Ax but 3c is useless; ~10-15% equity at best vs polarized c-bet range, far worse vs value-heavy. Pot odds 4:1 require ~20% equity, but calling OOP kills playability (no fold equity, stacks deep for implied odds on brick turns). Raising bluff terrible (bad blockers, board favors villain calls). Late tournament (137/150), deep effective stacks ~17k; folding preserves stack for better spots with position/equity. Standard check-fold maximizes EV.

Pocket Tens (TsTd) is an extremely strong hand heads-up, among the top starting hands pre-flop. As SB/Button, I have position and initiative. Opponent Flash (BB) has a larger stack (23050 vs my 16800) and history of calling small raises but folding to continued aggression. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is optimal sizing: builds the pot, gets value from worse hands, and applies pressure. Post-flop with position and pair, I'm in great shape against their likely wide calling range.

I am holding 2d 3c, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Elon has raised from the button to 300. Heads-up late in the tournament (hand 139/150) with deep stacks (~168BB effective). Button (Flash) opens wide from SB, typical range 40-60% of hands. Size to 900 (~3x the open) is standard, balances value (e.g., QQ+, AK) with bluffs. Pot odds make calling viable (+EV marginally), but 3bet maximizes EV by leveraging initiative and position post-3bet.

I am on the button with Q3o and opened for 300. Elon has 3-bet me to 900. While I am the chip leader with 22,750 chips compared to Elon's ~16,000, Q3o is a very weak hand to defend against a 3-bet. Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks late in the tournament (hand 140/150). A2o is a standard raising hand from SB vs BB heads-up due to ace blocker, decent playability, and position. Limping is too passive; folding too tight. Raise to standard 3x (300 total) to build stack by taking down small pots uncontested or playing profitably post-flop.

I am holding 8d 4d in the Big Blind. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button. With 10 hands remaining and a healthy chip lead, I'm getting 3:1 pot odds to see a flop with a suited hand. 84s has decent playability in a heads-up match, and folding here would be overly tight given the pot odds and Elon's frequent aggression. I have missed the flop completely with 8d 4d on a board of Td-Qc-7h. This board texture is likely to have connected with Elon's pre-flop raising range (Qx, Tx, and various straight draws). Heads-up deep on flop (140/150), pot 600, position, with Ad2c (ace-high, ace blocker to strong Ax hands). Flop Td Qc 7h is dry rainbow, favors my raising range (Qx, Tx, sets, overpairs). I have 8-high with no direct draw on a board that heavily favors Elon's pre-flop raising range (Qx, Tx, and various straight draws). I am holding Jack-Nine of diamonds on the button, which is a strong starting hand in heads-up play. Hand #141, heads-up, BB vs Button raise to 300 (3x). My hand 4h3c is extremely weak offsuit low cards with poor playability out of position. Button range is wide but still crushes this hand preflop. Near end of tournament (141/150), preserve stack for better spots. Standard fold.

Hand 142 of 150, heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (174bb effective). 6c4d is a weak offsuit hand but within a profitable stealing range from SB vs BB heads-up, where open-raise ranges are typically 50-70%+. KTo is a strong hand in heads-up play. Facing a button raise to 300, it is well worth defending the big blind. At this late stage of the tournament (Hand 142/150) and with a healthy chip lead, I'll call to see the flop and play post-flop.

On a board of Qc-Qd-As, I have King-high. This board heavily favors the pre-flop aggressor's range, as Elon will have many more Aces and Queens than I do after just calling from the Big Blind. I will check to him and see how he proceeds. Given the tournament stage and my chip lead, there is no need to play a bloated pot with no pair and no draw.

Heads-up on flop with initiative after preflop raise from SB/Button. Board Qc Qd As is dry, paired, and favors my PFR range which includes many Ax, Qx, and sets. On a board of Qc-Qd-As, my King-high has very little equity against Elon's range, which contains all the Aces and many Queens as the pre-flop aggressor. With 8-7 offsuit on the button, I have a standard opening hand in a heads-up or short-handed situation. Hand #143 pre-flop, heads-up. I hold 4s5d offsuit, a weak connector in BB facing a button min-raise to 300 (3x). Better spots available with deep stacks.

Heads-up preflop from the button/SB with Ah2s, an offsuit ace which is a standard raising hand in heads-up play due to its blocker value, high card strength, and playability postflop. I have Pocket Tens, which is a top-tier hand in heads-up play. Elon raised from the button, which is standard. I want to build a pot and extract value while I likely have the best hand. Preflop heads-up from BTN/SB with Ah2s facing a 3-bet to 1000 after my open to 300. Pot 1300, calling 700 into 2000 effective pot gives great odds (~3.2:1). Ah2s blocks villain's AA/AK/AQ value range significantly, has decent high-card strength, and strong playability postflop in position: ace-high can win unpaired boards, straight/flush potential with wheel/f suited connectors. The flop of King-Jack-Jack is quite unfavorable for my pocket Tens, as it contains two overcards that are heavily present in Elon's range for calling a 3-bet (such as K-Q, K-J, A-K, Q-J, or A-J). If he checks back, I might have the best hand or at least get a free card. If he bets, I will have to evaluate the size and determine if he is attempting to push me off a 3-bet range that missed this specific texture or if he actually has the King or Jack.

Flop board Kc Jd Jc is paired (JJ), with king high and club flush draw. I hold Pocket Tens, a strong pre-flop hand that I used to 3-bet. However, the flop of King-Jack-Jack is highly unfavorable for my specific holding. I hold K2o on the button and opened to 300. Elon has 3-bet to 900. I am in the Big Blind with 4s 2h. Elon, the button, has raised to 300. My hand is extremely weak (one of the worst in the game) and I am out of position. Hand 148 of 150, heads-up, SB/BTN vs BB. Q8o is a standard raise from SB heads-up due to position and wide ranges. Equity vs BB calling range is favorable (~55-60%). Hand 148/150, heads-up deep stacks (~190BB). Facing BB 3bet to 1000 after my SB open to 300 with Q8o offsuit. Position postflop crucial. The flop of Qh-Ks-Ac is extremely unfavorable for my pocket eights. Hand 148/150, heads-up, deep stacks (~180BB), IP on flop after calling BB 3bet pre with Q8o. Flop Qh Ks Ac rainbow: hits Q for bottom pair mediocre kicker, backdoor spade draw. Board dry but high cards favor villain's 3bet range heavily (AA/KK/AK/AQ/KQ, sets, str8 draws). Villain checks OOP, weak or trapping. Equity poor (~15-25% vs value range, better vs polarized). The turn 9d is another card that likely helps my opponent's calling range more than my pocket eights. Hand 148/150, heads-up deep stacks (~180BB), IP on turn after mutual flop checks with Q8s (pair of queens, weak kicker, backdoor spade). Board Qh Ks Ac 9d: dryish but villain's BB 3bet range heavily favors Ax/Kx (AK/AQ/Ace-high), QQ/KK/AA. Equity ~20-30% vs value-heavy range post-checks, better vs bluffs/draws. History (e.g., hands 144,136) shows value-betting nuts wins big, marginal spots control pot.

Hand 148/150, heads-up, deep stacks (~180BB), IP on river after mutual checks all streets OOP with Q8o flopping top pair weak kicker. Board Qh Ks Ac 9d Ts: scary with broadway straights (JT), two-pair combos (AK/AQ/KQ), sets (AA/KK/QQ), but villain's BB 3bet-check-check-check-check profile screams weakness (missed Ax/Kx/draws/bluffs per history). Bluff unnecessary (he checks weak); check induces bluffs/showdowns value hands cheaply, realizes equity optimally, preserves stack to steal blinds/manufacture pots in 149-150. History confirms passive OOP post-3bet, check dominant.

With only two hands remaining in the tournament and being nearly even in chips with Elon, my priority is to avoid unnecessary chip loss with a weak hand like 8-3 offsuit. This is the final hand #150 in the tournament.
